answer:
MrScraper uses token-based authentication for all API requests. For most endpoints, you must include your API token in the HTTP request headers using the key x-api-token [1][2].

For example:
```
x-api-token: YOUR_API_TOKEN
```

A specific subset of endpoints, namely those for analytics such as /analytic/statuses and /analytic/timeline, requires you to pass your token as a query parameter instead of a header [1]. For these endpoints, use the following parameter:

```
apiTokenName=YOUR_API_TOKEN
```

Always store your API token securely and avoid exposing it in client-side code [3].
